summary: Gruber systematically walks through each of the Ten Commandments — which Louisiana's new law requires to be displayed in all public classrooms — and matches each one against Donald Trump's well-documented history of violating it. Trump publicly endorsed the law on Truth Social, calling it 'the first major step in the revival of religion.' Gruber lets the irony do the heavy lifting, citing news reports for nine of the ten commandments that Trump has plausibly broken, giving him only a pass on honoring his father (with a sarcastic aside about Fred Trump's arrests). The post is structured as a listicle that pairs each commandment with sourced evidence of Trump's hypocrisy, requiring minimal editorial commentary because the juxtaposition speaks for itself.
key_points: Louisiana passed a law requiring the Ten Commandments to be displayed in all public classrooms, and Trump enthusiastically endorsed it as a religious revival
Gruber methodically pairs each commandment with documented instances of Trump violating it, from adultery to bearing false witness to coveting his neighbor's house
The post's structure — commandment, then sourced news clip — is itself the argument, requiring almost no editorializing
Gruber notes the law specifies the Protestant version of the Ten Commandments, not the Catholic version, highlighting the sectarian nature of the supposedly universal mandate
Trump's only passing grade is Commandment 5 (honor thy father), which Gruber undermines by noting Fred Trump's KKK arrest and racial discrimination lawsuit
money_quotes: Former president and convicted felon Donald Trump, on his floundering social network, approves.
Trump speaks highly of his father — from whom he inherited his handsome visage and enviable mane — so he's clear on this one.
'He is no longer president,' says one insider, 'so he doesn't have to go to church.'
Quite the man to honor, too.
key_insight: The man loudly championing the Ten Commandments in public schools has a well-documented public record of violating nearly every single one of them.
